board.unitAnchorRelationship: unit group origin is placed at the tile center; sprite body starts at y=0 and its visual foot/anchor is expected to meet that origin; pilot source anchors remain metadata at Archer 0.92, Slime 0.90, Golem 0.94

renderer.fullscreenCompatibility: document root fullscreen; relayout through GamePresentationHooks.relayout
renderer.screenshotCompatibility: fresh WebGL render copied without stretch/crop, then player HUD strip composited

themeRuntime.powerOfTwoRequirement: unresolved-no-final-file-loader-contract; current CanvasTexture helper does not assert dimensions
themeRuntime.pointerRule: theme decoration never joins tileMeshes or unit raycast targets

visualDirection.name: Map 1 — Arena Ruins
visualDirection.style: stylized 3D fantasy with hand-painted materials; polished mobile-game finish; never photorealistic
visualDirection.mood: ancient ruined fantasy arena, warm sand and light-brown stone with muted copper, battle wear and restrained cracks
visualDirection.combatPriority: units, HP bars, projectiles, target markers and tile states remain more salient than scenery
visualDirection.frequencyRule: low-frequency value grouping inside tile centers; seams, cracks and wear concentrate near grout and perimeter
visualDirection.lightingDirection: preserve existing warm key from world +x/+y/+z, cool fill from -x/+y/+z, and soft golden rim from far -z
forbidden: numbers or labels baked into art
bright saturated floor colors that outrank units

assetDeliverables.role: single full-board hand-painted stone surface covering the locked 8x7 runtime footprint, including visual treatment for the bench row without changing its logic

safeMargin.rule: keep the outer 32 px free of focal cracks/runes so border skin can overlap cleanly

reviewCriteria: bench row is distinct but not a new layout
no character shadow or state overlay baked in

assetDeliverables.role: low-profile broken masonry edge and corner modules for the locked stage rim

safeMargin.rule: 24 px transparent atlas perimeter and 16 px bleed between packed modules

runtimeReplacementTarget.integrationStatus: planned-not-implemented; atlas UV layout required after art approval

assetDeliverables.role: seamless low-contrast stone floor surrounding the arena; supplies depth without competing with the board

safeMargin.rule: opposite edges must tile seamlessly; no unique landmark inside the edge bands

reviewCriteria: no visible repeat at mobile scale
no baked vignette that breaks resize/fullscreen

assetDeliverables.role: distant ruined amphitheatre walls, arches and collapsed wall modules for back and side placement only

safeMargin.rule: transparent atlas perimeter with 16 px extrusion/bleed around modules

runtimeReplacementTarget.integrationStatus: planned-not-implemented; atlas UV layout required after art approval

reviewCriteria: no opaque module crosses board view
fantasy arena rather than generic city ruins
back silhouette stays below HP-bar risk envelope

assetDeliverables.role: nine transparent visual-state tiles; a visual contract only and not gameplay logic

safeMargin.rule: 16 px transparent gutter inside every 256x256 atlas cell

runtimeReplacementTarget.integrationStatus: planned-not-implemented; runtime currently exposes only normal/placement-valid/placement-invalid material states

reviewCriteria: all nine states remain distinct on a 128 px tile and at mobile downscale
selected/target/AoE do not resemble combat projectiles

assetDeliverables.role: broken columns, rubble, torn banners, brazier shells and restrained static ambient accents; dust/glow remain runtime VFX

safeMargin.rule: transparent atlas perimeter with 16 px extrusion/bleed around each module

tileAlignment.placement: outside full board, bench, HUD and touch zones

runtimeReplacementTarget.integrationStatus: planned-not-implemented; atlas UV layout required after art approval

reviewCriteria: no prop is required inside a logical tile
brazier shell has no baked animated glow

nonBitmapRuntimeLayers.ambientVfx: keep current slow dust/light-shaft runtime profile; static scuffs/runes belong in boardSurface and props; no VFX asset is produced by this plan
nonBitmapRuntimeLayers.lightingProfile: keep current additive cool fill and golden rim contract; no LUT or baked-light texture is required

memoryBudget.note: PNG download size is content-dependent and must be measured after production; GPU budgeting assumes every texture expands to RGBA8. Mipmaps would add about 33% and are not budgeted because the final loader/filter contract is unresolved.

tileStatePalette.edgeTreatment: soft inset seam, never a hard debug grid
tileStatePalette.mobileReadability: visible as a board rhythm, subordinate to units

tileStatePalette.edgeTreatment: one-pixel-equivalent inner bevel at final mobile scale
tileStatePalette.mobileReadability: must differ from normal without relying on cursor shape

tileStatePalette.edgeTreatment: double inset edge with quiet center
tileStatePalette.mobileReadability: subtle 5-8 percent luminance pulse allowed; edge remains readable when motion is disabled
distinct from Archer olive through higher value and lower yellow content

tileStatePalette.edgeTreatment: broken inner edge plus diagonal corner cuts; no text or X glyph
tileStatePalette.mobileReadability: shape treatment remains distinguishable under red-green color deficiency
zone reads across rows without tinting units solid red

tileStatePalette.edgeTreatment: radial corner brackets with clear center
tileStatePalette.mobileReadability: must remain distinct from cyan projectiles and HP bars

tileStatePalette.edgeTreatment: shared soft perimeter with reduced internal seams
tileStatePalette.mobileReadability: adjacent tiles must visually merge into one area without hiding tile boundaries

tileStatePalette.edgeTreatment: desaturated matte veil and quiet dashed edge
tileStatePalette.mobileReadability: reads as inactive without becoming an opaque obstacle

rules: no numbers or hard debug grid

layerProductionPlan.staticArt: seamless perimeter ground + background modules atlas
none; restrained static scuffs can be assigned to boardSurface/props
layerProductionPlan.runtimeOnly: dust, rare shafts, motion and degradation

layerProductionPlan.staticArt: none; paint materials to accept the locked light direction

productionOrder.name: board surface master
productionOrder.approvalGate: surface value grouping, 8x7 alignment and pilot-foot quiet zones approved before Pass 2

productionOrder.name: border and corners
productionOrder.approvalGate: front/side occlusion and corner fit approved before Pass 3

productionOrder.name: perimeter/background
productionOrder.approvalGate: board-background separation and fullscreen repeat behavior approved before Pass 4

productionOrder.name: tile readability overlays
productionOrder.approvalGate: all nine states approved at 1x mobile scale before Pass 5

productionOrder.name: ambient props
productionOrder.approvalGate: prop no-go-zone compliance approved before Pass 6

productionOrder.name: mobile readability and pilot-unit contrast review
productionOrder.approvalGate: all three future checkpoints completed; this plan makes no pass claim

reviewCheckpoints.name: Board Readability
criteria: combat area free of noisy detail

unresolved: The brief says 8x8, but exact base runtime defines GRID_COLS=8 and GRID_ROWS=7 with 56 tiles; row 6 is the integrated bench. Because layout is locked, production targets 8x7 until an owner changes the brief or runtime in a separate scope.

MapThemeRuntime has a CanvasTexture draw helper but no external final-file loader, URL/path registry, atlas UV contract, or colorSpace assignment; final integration details remain unresolved and are not implemented here.
The perimeter ground is currently owned by src/game.js, not mapThemeRoot/background. Its future ownership/replacement seam requires an explicit integration decision; this plan does not move it.

Runtime currently implements normal, deploy-valid and deploy-invalid tile material behavior only. Hover, selected, enemy-zone, target, AoE and disabled are visual-contract entries for future integration and do not imply gameplay support.
No explicit renderOrder is assigned to the six theme groups; the documented layer list is insertion/build order, not a guaranteed GPU draw order for transparent meshes.

Exact screen-space UI rectangles vary with DOM content, breakpoints, safe-area insets and fullscreen. Safe zones use conservative normalized risk bands and require future browser measurement at the three review checkpoints.
